summary: Decompiled a large portion of src/melee/lb/lbmthp, the THP/movie playback library unit. The PR replaced many placeholder functions with C implementations for THP frame-buffer setup, asynchronous DevCom streaming callbacks, frame advancement, decode/update logic, texture upload/render setup, movie start/stop, and one-shot THP image decode. It also expanded lbmthp.h and lbmthp.static.h with more accurate THPDecComp and static Movieplayer layout fields, THP decoder prototypes, OSAlarm/GXTexObj members, and renamed the static global from lbl_804333E0 to Movieplayer. Automated decomp.dev output reported 11 unmatched-item improvements, including several functions rising from 0% to high match percentages and .data improving from 24.85% to 91.42%. Human review evidence is minimal: the PR body only says it is a tracking PR and there are no review comments.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; added C bodies for numerous previously stubbed lbmthp/THP movie playback functions and switched existing accessors from lbl_804333E0 to Movieplayer.
Public/internal header updates; added THP decoder prototypes and filled in THPDecComp fields used by the new implementations.
Static-layout header; expanded struct lbl_804333E0_t with Movieplayer fields such as frame buffers, stream indices, current packed size, OSAlarm, GXTexObj objects, and power flag.

what_changed: Implemented fn_8001E910 as a DevCom completion/callback-style routine that records OS tick deltas, advances packed-frame offsets and ring-buffer indices, asserts nonzero currPackedSize, and queues HSD_DevComRequest when playback/streaming conditions allow.

Implemented fn_8001ECF4 to initialize frame pointer tables and Y/U/V output buffers inside a caller-provided buffer, preload packed frame data through lbFile_800161C4, align packed sizes to 32 bytes, invalidate cache ranges, and emit diagnostic OSReport output before an assert on invalid zero sizes.

Implemented fn_8001EF5C to decode the current THP frame using THPVideoDecode under interrupt protection, convert decoded output through THPDec_80331340 for 0x280-wide frames or THPDec_803313D0 otherwise, store cache ranges for the 0x280 path, update decoded-frame state, and then request the next stream read via fn_8001F13C.
Implemented fn_8001F13C to schedule the next asynchronous packed-frame read when the stream is active, not already busy, and not caught up to the decode index.

Implemented fn_8001F2A4 as the periodic alarm handler/frame advancement routine, including repeated conversion from tick count to frame number using an optional rate table and loop/end-of-movie handling via Movieplayer.unk_68 and Movieplayer.unk_144.

Implemented lbMthp_8001F410 to start movie playback: assert the player is not already powered, read/init THP metadata, compute required memory, use caller memory or allocate via HSD_MemAlloc, set looping, initialize buffers, create an OS alarm, and install fn_8001F2A4 as a periodic alarm handler.
Implemented lbMthp_8001F67C to decode/update a movie frame and, if rendering is enabled, initialize/load three GX I8 textures for Y, U, and V planes before calling HSD_SObjLib_803A49E0.

Implemented lbMthp8001FAA0 as a one-shot THP decode path that initializes THP, loads file data, allocates Y/U/V output planes and decoder context/buffer, calls THPVideoDecode, converts to planar outputs with width-dependent conversion routines, then frees temporary buffers.

Reworked lbMthp_8001F800, fn_8001F294, lbMthp_8001F87C, and small accessors to use named Movieplayer fields such as power, unk_70, unk_110, unk_140, and OSAlarm instead of byte-offset pointer arithmetic or lbl_804333E0 access.
Added needed includes for Runtime/runtime.h, MSL/string.h, OSCache, baselib debug/devcom, and OSAlarm support.
smart_moves: Renaming the static global to Movieplayer made many formerly opaque lbl_804333E0 field accesses easier to reason about without claiming all individual fields are semantically known.
The struct work was incremental and evidence-driven: fields that were needed by the new matching code were split out while still preserving unknown/pad regions and STATIC_ASSERT size checks.

The implementations preserve hardware-facing details that commonly affect matching and correctness on GameCube: OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts around shared decode/stream state, 32-byte alignment for DevCom/lbFile reads, and DCInvalidateRange/DCStoreRange for YUV planes.
For THP output conversion, the code captures the observed width-specialized branch: 0x280-wide frames use THPDec_80331340 and cache stores, while other widths use THPDec_803313D0.
The frame-rate/rate-table conversion logic in fn_8001F2A4 was kept explicit and repeated rather than over-abstracted, which likely helped preserve codegen shape for matching.
The PR updated headers and static headers alongside the C bodies, avoiding local-only guesses and making newly required THP decoder, OSAlarm, and GXTexObj types available to future work.
decomp_lessons: For decompiling streaming media code, identify ring-buffer indices, current packed size, file offset, busy flag, loop flag, and decoded-frame index before trying to simplify control flow; these fields drive both matching and correctness.
Avoid replacing repeated frame-number conversion blocks with helpers unless the original code clearly shared a helper; repeated source may be necessary for matching compiler output.
Cache management calls are not incidental in THP/GX code. DCInvalidateRange and DCStoreRange placement can be part of both functional behavior and matching.

Asynchronous file/device reads often require both interrupt masking and callback function signatures to match the surrounding library conventions. Here fn_8001E910 was typed as void fn_8001E910(int, int, void*, bool).
When global static structs are still mostly unknown, a useful middle ground is to name the global semantically, expose only proven fields, keep padding for the rest, and retain size assertions.
For YUV texture upload paths, expect three GX_TF_I8 texture objects: one full-size luma plane and two half-width/half-height chroma planes.
naming_conventions: The static global at 0x4333E0 was renamed from lbl_804333E0 to Movieplayer, while the underlying struct tag remained struct lbl_804333E0_t.
A field at offset 0x124 was given the semantic name currPackedSize, reflecting its use in aligned packed-frame reads and asserts.
A field at offset 0x14C was named power, replacing prior raw unk_14C usage in start/stop/power-state checks.
Most newly exposed fields remain offset-style unk_XX names, indicating insufficient evidence for stronger semantic names.
THPDecComp gained explicit field names for width and height at offsets 0x44 and 0x48, while many other fields are still unk_XX.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK additions in fn_8001EBF0 and fn_8001ECF4 to influence stack layout for matching.
Preserved explicit casts between u32, s32, pointers, and uintptr_t in DevCom and frame-index code to match signedness and codegen-sensitive comparisons.
Used ALIGN_32 or equivalent ((size + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0) expressions for packed THP read sizes.
Kept interrupt save/restore pairs around shared state changes and THPVideoDecode calls to reproduce the expected critical sections.

Used a local copy `struct lbl_804333E0_t streamPlayer = Movieplayer` inside fn_8001E910 before HSD_ASSERTREPORT, which appears to be a codegen/matching tactic rather than a semantic necessity based on the diff.
Maintained width-specific branches for THPDec_80331340 versus THPDec_803313D0 instead of generalizing the decode conversion path.

review_feedback.feedback: Only says "Tracking PR as usual"; no technical rationale was provided.

Reported 11 improvements in unmatched items for main/melee/lb/lbmthp, including fn_8001ECF4 to 94.05%, fn_8001E910 to 81.22%, .data to 91.42%, lbMthp8001FAA0 to 99.74%, fn_8001F13C to 99.98%, lbMthp_8001F410 to 95.41%, fn_8001F2A4 to 90.79%, lbMthp_8001F67C to 83.79%, fn_8001EF5C to 99.85%, .sdata2 to 83.33%, and fn_8001EBF0 from 98.63% to 98.77%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename THPDecComp unk_XX fields to semantic names after this lbmthp struct expansion.
Search for follow-up work improving fn_8001E910 and lbMthp_8001F67C match percentages beyond the 81-84% range reported here.
Compare other movie/THP-related decomp PRs for the same THPDec_80331340 versus THPDec_803313D0 width-dependent conversion pattern.
Look for later review or cleanup of the local struct copy in fn_8001E910 to confirm whether it was purely a matching workaround.
Search for PRs touching Movieplayer.power, currPackedSize, or lbmthp.static.h to track naming stabilization.
